items.headline: City Council discusses 2026-27 capital improvement budget
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects. This is the planning document that determines which physical improvements and projects the city will fund in the coming year.

items.headline: City Council to revise residential tenant protections rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance to amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program, which could affect renter rights and landlord obligations. Details on specific changes will be presented at the April 14 Housing and Human Services Commission meeting.

items.headline: City releases annual youth services and library facility plans
items.summary: The City Council reviewed status reports on the Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports outline the city's strategy for improving services for young people and library infrastructure.

items.headline: Downtown historic building eligible for major expansion
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street could be upgraded from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, allowing it to gain 5,000 additional square feet of floor area as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation. This affects what can be built downtown.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone two properties on San Antonio Road to build an 8-story building with 167 apartments (28 affordable units) and commercial space. The proposal requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing in that area.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled, pushing the next hearing to April 22. A development project at 333 Franklin Street will not be heard on the original date and will be re-noticed when rescheduled, delaying what may be a significant local development decision.

items.headline: City Council approves construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully described in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what infrastructure or development project this contract covers.

items.headline: City grants $X to Mexican Heritage Plaza School of Arts and Culture
items.summary: San José received a California Natural Resources Agency grant to support the School of Arts and Culture at Mexican Heritage Plaza. This funding will help expand arts and cultural programming in the city.
